title: Undo, Restore, Revert, Reset, and Reflog Recovery · Conflict Triage
summary: Recover from an incorrect edit or commit using the least destructive operation and a verified recovery reference. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in an undo decision matrix and disaster-recovery lab and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence de…
objectives: Select an undo operation from affected layer, publication state, collaboration, desired topology, and recovery evidence.
Use restore for working-tree and index paths with explicit sources, patch selection, and pre-change diffs.
Create and verify inverse commits for published changes, merges, and later dependencies without claiming history was erased.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-basics cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Inspect the failure record
steps.instruction:
debug case — One unstaged edit, one staged file, local commit a11ce00, and published commit b00b1e5 each need different recovery. Preserve a temporary ref and choose restore, reset, revert, or reflog per layer.

Inspect the Undo Layer Choice trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for select an undo operation from affected layer, publication state, collaboration, desired topology, and recovery evidence.

content.text: For Undo Layer Choice, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

hints: Name the Undo Layer Choice precondition or invariant that the debug case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: One unstaged edit, one staged file, local commit a11ce00, and published commit b00b1e5 each need different recovery. Preserve a temporary ref and choose restore, reset, revert, or reflog per layer.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: There is one Git undo command that safely applies to every mistake.

stimulus.title: Undo Layer Choice case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: One unstaged edit, one staged file, local commit a11ce00, and published commit b00b1e5 each need different recovery. Preserve a temporary ref and choose restore, reset, revert, or reflog per layer.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: There is one Git undo command that safely applies to every mistake.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Select an undo operation from affected layer, publication state, collaboration, desired topology, and recovery evidence. Artifact under review: an undo decision matrix and disaster-recovery lab.

options.text: Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
The record names the assumption, applies Undo Layer Choice, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample.

steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for use reflogs, dangling-object inspection, temporary references, and expiry limits to recover otherwise unreachable work.

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair
steps.instruction: Append a safe repository investigation headed "# Evidence: git-reflog-object-recovery-1vdugl" with a concrete hypothesis, one bounded simulator command, and an observation that changes the next decision.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Reflog Object Recovery into observable performance evidence.

options.text: Inspect repository boundary, config origin, current operation, worktree, index, refs, and graph
Preserve uncommitted work and a recovery ref, then state intended tree and topology
Preview and perform the least destructive simulated Git operation
Verify status, staged and range diffs, graph, tests, remote target, and recovery path

steps.title: Publish the incident record
steps.instruction: Write the debug evidence note for Reflog Object Recovery: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — One unstaged edit, one staged file, local commit a11ce00, and published commit b00b1e5 each need different recovery. Preserve a temporary ref and choose restore, reset, revert, or reflog per layer.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Use reflogs, dangling-object inspection, temporary references, and expiry limits to recover otherwise unreachable work.
